rules.description: Forbid inline style attributes; styling lives in stylesheets.
rules.what: Flags style= attributes in HTML/JSX/Vue/Svelte markup.
rules.why: One styling channel keeps design tokens consistent and themeable.

rules.description: Every HTML document declares exactly one primary heading.
rules.what: Flags HTML files with zero or more than one <h1>.
rules.why: A single H1 fixes one canonical document outline for a11y and SEO.

rules.description: A directory uses one filename case convention, never a mix.
rules.what: Flags directories that mix snake_case and kebab-case filenames.
rules.why: One naming convention removes bikeshedding and import ambiguity.

rules.description: A module exposes a bounded surface so responsibilities stay small.
rules.what: Flags modules whose export count exceeds the configured maximum.
rules.why: A small public surface keeps modules single-purpose and composable.

rules.description: User-facing UI should feel alive, not like a thin shell.
rules.what: Flags UI files that hide controls behind dead buttons or unlabeled inputs.
rules.why: Design-be-gone should force interfaces to feel complete and human.

rules.what: Flags <button> elements without data-action or onclick wiring.
rules.why: A button without an action is fake affordance.
Implicit button types make forms feel brittle.

rules.what: Flags inputs, textareas, and selects with no label or accessible name.
rules.why: A readable interface always tells users what each control means.

rules.what: Flags data-action hooks that no script resolves.
rules.why: Wired UI should map each action to a real implementation.
